title: ProgramBench: Can Language Models Rebuild Programs From Scratch?
domain: Software engineering agents; code generation benchmarks; behavioral program reconstruction
mainContribution: Introduces ProgramBench, a 200-task benchmark where software engineering agents must rebuild executable behavior from only a compiled program and its documentation, evaluated by hidden behavioral tests generated through agent-driven fuzzing.
datasets: ProgramBench generated tests on Hugging Face
notes: Paper PDF was supplied in the repository root and moved to this folder as paper.pdf after implementation files were created.
The paper reports no model fully resolving any of the 200 tasks; live leaderboard values may change after paper release.
External repository issues found shortly after release document cleanroom-image documentation gaps, a readable injected executable in one task image, and a Go toolchain mismatch.
